mnemonics.title: ARM Cortex-M Vector Table Order
mnemonics.content: Memory layout: ISP - Reset, ISP - NMI, ISP - HardFault, ISP - MemManage, ISP - BusFault, ISP - UsageFault, 0, ISP - SVCall, ISP - DebugMon, 0, ISP - PendSV, ISP - SysTick, ISP - IRQ0-IRQ59. Mnemonic: 'R Never Marries Because Uncles See Some Dumb Nuns Pondering Over Interrupt Requests' (Reset-NMI-HardFault-MemManage-BusFault-UsageFault-SVCall-DebugMon-PendSV-SysTick-IRQs).

mnemonics.title: Thumb Instruction Benefits
mnemonics.content: T - Tiny (16-bit), H - High density, U - Useful (compatibility), M - Memory efficient, B - Bytes saved. Thumb = Tiny, High-density, Useful, Memory-efficient Thumb.

mnemonics.title: Bootloader Functions
mnemonics.content: I - Initialize hardware, N - Load application, I - Initialize memory, T - Transfer control. BASIC: Basic Initialization Control. Mnemonic: 'Boot Sees Initialized Code Transferring' (Basic, Startup, Initialize, Copy, Transfer).

acronyms.usage: Processor architecture family for embedded systems
CPU design philosophy with simple, fast instructions
Standard software interface for Cortex-M processors
R13 register pointing to top of stack
R15 register storing next instruction address

visualTips.visualization: Think of vector table as a 'first responder directory' - first entry (address 0) is emergency contact for stack, second is who to call when system resets, then priority list of all interrupt handlers in order.
ARM = 4-lane highway (32-bit), Thumb = 2-lane but more fuel-efficient (16-bit). Thumb-2 = smart car that can use both roads.
Bootloader is like a stage manager - sets up lights (hardware), ensures actors (code) are in right positions (memory), then calls 'action' (jumps to main).
MPU is like a security guard with a visitor pass system - defines zones, checks permissions, prevents unauthorized access to restricted areas.

analogies.analogy: Like a BIOS in computers that runs POST tests, initializes hardware, and then loads the operating system.
Like a universal remote - provides same buttons (API) regardless of TV brand (hardware), translating your press to specific device actions.
Like an emergency contact list pinned on refrigerator - tells whom to call for each type of emergency (interrupt).
Like an architect's floor plan - specifies where each room (code section) goes in the building (memory).
